description: Visualize and edit WinForms .Designer.cs forms live inside VS Code — a Visual Studio-style designer surface, property grid, toolbox and event wiring, powered by a .NET 9 rendering engine.

configuration.title: WinForms Designer
untrusted.description: Rendering a designer loads and executes the project's control assemblies (constructors / OnPaint run on preview), so the extension is disabled in untrusted workspaces.
config.autoOpenDesigner.description: Automatically open the WinForms designer when a form's `.cs` (one that has a generated `.Designer.cs` partner) becomes the active editor — mirroring Visual Studio, where opening `Form1.cs` shows the designer. Use **View Code** to switch a form back to its `.cs` text (auto-open won't re-hijack it until you reopen the file). Disable this to keep the C# text editor and open the designer manually via **WinForms: Open Designer** or *Reopen Editor With…*.
config.assemblyPath.description:
Explicit path to the project's built control assembly (`.dll`) used to resolve custom/3rd-party controls. Leave empty to auto-discover the project's build output. Set this only when auto-discovery fails (e.g. multi-target, a custom `OutputPath`, or a not-yet-built project).

- A leading `~` expands to your home directory; a relative path resolves against the workspace folder (or the file's own directory when no folder is open). Environment variables (`%VAR%`, `${env:VAR}`, `$VAR`) are **not** expanded.
- If the resolved path does not exist, a warning is shown and auto-discovery is used instead.
- **The assembly is loaded and its control constructors / `OnPaint` run when a designer is previewed**, so it is honored only in a trusted workspace.
config.language.description:
Language of the WinForms Designer user interface — the designer surface, property grid, toolbox, dialogs and messages. This is chosen **here**, in the extension settings; it does **not** follow the VS Code display language automatically. Default: **English**.

- Changing this takes full effect after **Reload Window** (you'll be prompted).
- Note: the VS Code **command palette** titles (e.g. *WinForms: Open Designer*) and this settings page itself follow VS Code's own *Display Language*, not this setting — a platform limitation.
